rationale: The citing paper uses Klink and Smith (2001) to motivate its core positioning that brand extension research needs greater external validity and more marketplace-realistic conditions. It cites this work in the contribution framing and later explicitly states that the present research responds to that call. This gives the cited paper a clear conceptual role in defining the problem space, even though the new studies do not directly replicate or test Klink and Smith's specific manipulations.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Klink and Smith (2001) as a conceptual foundation for why brand extension findings should be examined under more realistic conditions. It builds its contribution around addressing that external-validity agenda via life-role transitions as a consumer-state driver of distant extension evaluations.
